objective: Evaluate system progress against a predefined multi-phase execution architecture and convert the entire interaction into a structured forensic JSON state
decisions: Execution flow defined from Phase 0 to Final with strict order
System must not skip phases during development
Progress evaluation mapped across all phases with completion percentages
Phase 3 (Workflow + Orchestration) identified as immediate priority
Phase 4 (Intelligence + Decision + AI) to follow after Phase 3 completion
Execution requires clarification of orchestration model (DAG, state machine, event-driven, or hybrid)
Forensic extraction mode selected due to absence of JSON input
Strict rules enforced: no assumptions, no loose summarization, full traceability
built_components: Partial execution engine logic (plan execution awareness)
Redis-based state awareness (conceptual or partial implementation)
inputs: User instruction for forensic JSON conversion
Strict system rules for extraction and execution modes
outputs: Identification of completed, partial, and missing phases
Risk identification for system scalability and structure
dependencies: Potential queue systems (Celery, RQ, Kafka not implemented)
notes: System is in a mid-architecture state with strong governance and structural definition (Phase 0–1), partial execution capability (Phase 2–3), and conceptual intelligence layer (Phase 4). Later phases (5–8 and Final) are not started. No orchestration engine, job lifecycle system, or deployment infrastructure exists. Execution is currently blocked on orchestration model definition.
